title: Committed artifacts and canonical queue authorization are separate temporal layers

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f records M-001 as completed and contains no later continuity-owned item.

The default branch contains commits 8d5a39d15683c11813f5b28d40f17b8e680822f5, c4d7571bb3267e6a383beac2c4ad9f0bbd93aa70, 1f815d92a5085451cc1f83a19e05b8232c6068e7, and eb92c563af4c3abf4676cae8b1724392c8fb4087 for the queue-promotion reconciliation gate, tests, evidence, and handoff.
The continuity index already requires historical observations to be superseded rather than erased when queue state changes.
inferred: Canonical queue state is temporally behind the most recent committed continuity work.
Repository existence proves an artifact was committed, but does not by itself prove that the artifact is canonically authorized as executable queue work.
proposed: Every run should record both the observed queue blob and the newest relevant commit boundary.
A committed artifact absent from the canonical queue should be classified as committed_unpromoted until an owned queue transition explicitly incorporates it.
superseded: The earlier interpretation that the queue-promotion artifacts were absent from repository history is superseded by direct observation of the four commits on the default branch.
unresolved: No canonical M-002 or later continuity item currently promotes the queue-promotion gate into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
The repository does not yet expose one atomic mechanism that updates artifact evidence and canonical queue authorization together.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the exact queue and continuity-index blobs, then fetch all four commits. Reject any observed statement whose path, blob, or commit cannot be independently resolved. Resolve committed_unpromoted only through a later canonical queue blob that explicitly incorporates the work.
